    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
    // UploadAsync — uploaded file name sanitization (path traversal / arbitrary
    // file write hardening).
    //
    // The client controls IFormFile.FileName, so it must be treated as
    // untrusted. A malicious name like "../../escape.txt" or an absolute path
    // must NOT cause a file to be written outside the home root. These tests
    // pin the post-fix contract: any traversal/absolute/invalid name either is

    /// <summary>
    /// The single most important regression: a traversal sequence in the
    /// uploaded file name must not place a file outside the home root. The
    /// vulnerable code path combines the raw <c>file.FileName</c> with the
    /// resolved directory, so <c>"../escape.txt"</c> resolves to the
    /// content root (one level above Home) and a file is created there.
    /// </summary>
    [Fact]
    public async Task UploadAsync_FileNameWithParentTraversal_DoesNotWriteOutsideRoot()
    {

        // The exact OS-resolved location the vulnerable implementation would
        // write to: Path.Combine(root, "../escape.txt") -> parent of root.

        {

        }
        catch (ArgumentException)
        {
            // A rejecting implementation is also acceptable; the assertion
            // below is the real contract.
        }

        Assert.False(File.Exists(escapeTarget),
            $"file was written outside the home root at {escapeTarget}");
    }

    // strictly inside the root — but in NO case may a file appear outside it.

    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------

    /// <summary>
    /// The headline regression: a parent-traversal sequence in the uploaded
    /// file name must never write outside the home root. The vulnerable
    /// <c>Path.Combine(root, "../escape.txt")</c> resolves one level above
    /// <c>Home</c> (into the content root) and creates a file there.
    /// </summary>
    [Fact]
    public async Task UploadAsync_FileNameWithParentTraversal_DoesNotWriteOutsideRoot()
    {
        var (service, root) = CreateService();
        // The OS-resolved location the vulnerable code would write to.
        var escapeTarget = Path.GetFullPath(Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt"));
        Assert.False(File.Exists(escapeTarget));

        var file = FormFileFactory.CreateFormFile("../escape.txt", "x");
        try
        {
            await service.UploadAsync("", file);
        }
        catch (ArgumentException)
        {
            // A rejecting implementation also satisfies the contract; the
            // assertion below is the real guarantee.
        }

        Assert.False(File.Exists(escapeTarget),
            $"uploaded file escaped the home root to {escapeTarget}");
    }
